meta.purpose: Lock official NORS training and curriculum sources for future crosswalk QA. NORS guidance controls reporting and complaint coding; regulations, Appendix PP, and CT overlays provide authority and context.

source_pack_rows.title: NORS Table 1: Case and Complaint Codes, Values, and Definitions
source_pack_rows.preferred_use: Primary reference for case-level coding, including case values, verification, disposition, referral fields, and case closure values.
source_pack_rows.page_or_section_note: Table 1; used with NORS Part 1 and Part 3 training.
source_pack_rows.limitations: Does not teach workflow by itself; use with NORS Part 1 and Part 3 training.
source_pack_rows.notes: Source pack baseline for case-level NORS reporting values.

source_pack_rows.title: NORS Table 2: Complaint Codes and Definitions (10-31-2024)
source_pack_rows.preferred_use: Primary reference for NORS complaint code labels, definitions, examples, and reporting tips.
source_pack_rows.limitations: Provides complaint code definitions and examples, but not the full case workflow; use with NORS Parts 1-4 training.
source_pack_rows.notes: Production truth for NORS minor-code labels and definitions.

source_pack_rows.title: NORS Table 3: State Program Information
source_pack_rows.preferred_use: Primary reference for state program information and activity reporting codes used with NORS Part 4.
source_pack_rows.limitations: Does not cover complaint coding.
source_pack_rows.notes: Source pack baseline for program activity reporting values.

source_pack_rows.title: National Ombudsman Reporting System (NORS) Hub
source_pack_rows.preferred_use: Primary hub for checking current NORS tables, training materials, FAQs, decision trees, and updates.
source_pack_rows.page_or_section_note: NORS resources page; lists resources updated as of July 2025.
source_pack_rows.limitations: Index page; use direct linked documents for specific coding rules.
source_pack_rows.notes: Use this page to detect later source-pack updates.

source_pack_rows.title: NORS Training Part 1: Case, Complaint, Complainant, Information and Assistance - Basic Principles
source_pack_rows.preferred_use: Primary training authority for distinguishing cases from information and assistance, counting complaints, and identifying the complainant before complaint coding.
source_pack_rows.limitations: Does not define individual complaint codes; use Table 2 and Part 2 for complaint coding.

source_pack_rows.title: NORS Training Part 1: Quiz
source_pack_rows.preferred_use: Scenario practice for deciding case vs I&A, number of complaints, and complainant.
source_pack_rows.page_or_section_note: Use with Part 1 basic principles and answer sheet.
source_pack_rows.limitations: Training practice; answer sheet controls interpretation of scenarios.

source_pack_rows.title: NORS Training Part 1: Quiz Answer Sheet (Revised 2025)
source_pack_rows.preferred_use: High-value QA source for ambiguous case vs I&A and complainant scenarios.
source_pack_rows.limitations: Training answer sheet; interpret with ACL Tables 1-3 and Part 1 principles.

source_pack_rows.title: NORS Training Part 2: Complaint Coding Basic Principles
source_pack_rows.preferred_use: Primary training authority for complaint-coding principles, including one complaint equals one complaint code and A-M vs N-Q categories.
source_pack_rows.limitations: Predates later table revisions; apply together with current ACL Table 2.
source_pack_rows.notes: Use for complaint coding logic after Part 1 case/complaint screening.

source_pack_rows.title: NORS Training Part 2: Complaint Coding Webinar Slides
source_pack_rows.preferred_use: Slide-based reinforcement of Part 2 complaint-coding principles and Table 2 usage.
source_pack_rows.limitations: Summarizes core rules; use with Basic Principles and Table 2.

source_pack_rows.title: NORS Training Part 2: Complaint Coding Quiz
source_pack_rows.preferred_use: Scenario practice for complaint coding using Table 2 and Part 2 principles.
source_pack_rows.limitations: Training practice; answer sheet controls intended interpretation.

source_pack_rows.title: NORS Training Part 2: Complaint Coding Quiz Answer Sheet
source_pack_rows.preferred_use: High-value QA source showing how NORC resolves ambiguous complaint-coding scenarios.
source_pack_rows.limitations: Interpret with current ACL Table 2 and Part 2 basic principles.
source_pack_rows.notes: Use for ambiguity rules such as abuse vs poor care, rights vs care, and outside-agency routing.

source_pack_rows.title: NORS Training Part 2: Complaint Coding Beyond the Basics Quiz Answer Sheet (2021)
source_pack_rows.preferred_use: Advanced scenario guidance for multi-issue cases and outside-agency/system complaint distinctions.
source_pack_rows.limitations: Prefer revised 2025 answer sheet where there is a conflict.

source_pack_rows.title: NORS Training Part 2: Complaint Coding Beyond the Basics Quiz Answer Sheet (Revised)
source_pack_rows.preferred_use: Most recent advanced answer set; preferred training reference when it differs from older beyond-basics materials.
source_pack_rows.limitations: Assumes familiarity with Part 2 principles and ACL tables.

source_pack_rows.title: NORS Training Part 3: Verification, Disposition, Referral, and Closing Cases Basic Principles
source_pack_rows.preferred_use: Primary training authority for verification, disposition, referral, and closing cases.
source_pack_rows.page_or_section_note: Hub page for revised NORS data-collection materials; direct current PDF URL should be confirmed.
source_pack_rows.limitations: Hub URL; direct Part 3 source URL needs periodic manual check.

source_pack_rows.title: NORS Training Part 3: Verification, Disposition, Referral, and Closing Cases Quiz Answer Sheet
source_pack_rows.preferred_use: QA examples for verification, disposition, referral, and case-closing logic.
source_pack_rows.limitations: Use with ACL Table 1 and Part 3 principles.

source_pack_rows.title: NORS Training Part 4: Ombudsman Program Activities Basic Principles
source_pack_rows.preferred_use: Primary training authority for program activity reporting in NORS.
source_pack_rows.limitations: Activity reporting, not complaint coding.

source_pack_rows.title: NORS FAQs
source_pack_rows.preferred_use: Clarifies ambiguous or commonly misunderstood NORS rules.
source_pack_rows.page_or_section_note: NORS FAQ page; listed as updated July 2025 on NORS hub.
source_pack_rows.limitations: Clarifies tables and training; does not override ACL tables.

source_pack_rows.title: Online NORS Training Course Launch - Webinar Slides
source_pack_rows.preferred_use: Orientation to the online NORS course and current source pack.
source_pack_rows.limitations: Overview only; detailed coding rules remain in tables and Part 1-4 materials.

source_pack_rows.title: Initial Certification Training Curriculum for Long-Term Care Ombudsman Programs - Modules 1-10
source_pack_rows.preferred_use: Broad initial certification curriculum supporting resident-first complaint processing, documentation, roles, and workflow.
source_pack_rows.limitations: Broader than NORS reporting; does not override NORS tables or Parts 1-4 for coding.

source_pack_rows.title: Ombudsman Training - NORC Training Resources Page
source_pack_rows.preferred_use: Index for NORC training resources; useful for update checks.
source_pack_rows.limitations: Pointer resource only.

source_pack_rows.title: NORS Consistency and Clarifications - Virtual Office Hour
source_pack_rows.preferred_use: Interpretive guidance for common NORS consistency questions and QA edge cases.
source_pack_rows.limitations: Clarification only; does not create new coding rules.

source_relationship_rows.relationship_note: ACL NORS Tables 1-3 are the foundational data definitions for NORS training Parts 1-4.
Part 1 case/complaint/I&A screening should be applied before Part 2 complaint coding.
Part 2 explains how to use Table 2; Table 2 supplies complaint code labels, definitions, examples, and reporting tips.
Part 2 quizzes and answer sheets show NORC's intended coding choices for ambiguous scenarios.
Part 3 training and Table 1 control verification, disposition, referral, and case-closing values.
Part 4 training and Table 3 control program activity reporting.
NORS FAQs clarify and expand on questions that arise from Parts 1-4.
Initial certification curriculum supports workflow and documentation, but NORS-specific training controls reporting codes.
The 2025 NORS course launch confirms the current online course and revised NORS training source set.

source_priority_rules.rule: ACL NORS Tables 1-3 and NORC NORS Training Parts 1-4 are the controlling sources for NORS reporting and complaint coding.
source_priority_rules.plain_language_rationale: ACL tables define NORS values and NORC training teaches how to apply them. All complaint coding and case reporting decisions start here.

source_priority_rules.rule: CMS Appendix PP and F-tags provide regulatory and surveyor context but do not determine final NORS complaint codes.
source_priority_rules.plain_language_rationale: NORS is an ombudsman reporting system, not a survey tool. F-tags can explain rights and facility obligations after likely NORS issues are identified.

source_priority_rules.rule: Part 1 sources are used first to distinguish cases from information and assistance, count complaints, and identify the complainant.
source_priority_rules.plain_language_rationale: Before coding a complaint, the user must know whether the contact is a case or I&A and how many complaints exist.

source_priority_rules.rule: Part 2 sources are primary for complaint coding and ambiguity rules once a case and complaint have been identified.
source_priority_rules.plain_language_rationale: Part 2 and Table 2 control complaint-code selection. Answer sheets guide ambiguous phrases and scenarios.

source_priority_rules.rule: Part 3 sources are primary for verification, disposition, referral, and closing cases.
source_priority_rules.plain_language_rationale: Part 3 explains how to apply Table 1 fields after complaint work is completed.

source_priority_rules.rule: Part 4 sources are primary for program activity reporting.
source_priority_rules.plain_language_rationale: Program activities are not complaint codes; Table 3 and Part 4 control this reporting layer.

source_priority_rules.rule: FAQs and quiz answer sheets are high-value QA sources for ambiguous scenarios but must be interpreted in light of ACL tables and basic principles.
source_priority_rules.plain_language_rationale: FAQs and answer sheets show how NORC resolves hard cases in practice; they are QA aids below ACL tables and basic principles.

source_priority_rules.rule: Initial certification curriculum modules support resident-first workflow and documentation but do not override NORS tables or NORS Parts 1-4 for reporting codes.
source_priority_rules.plain_language_rationale: The curriculum shapes how staff investigate and document. NORS-specific sources still control reporting codes.

human_review_flags.issue: Some NORS training URLs are hub pages that may link to updated PDFs over time.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Periodically verify that linked Part 1-4 PDFs and quizzes have not been replaced or renumbered.

human_review_flags.issue: Publication dates for some quizzes and answer sheets are not clearly listed on the documents.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Before a formal production release, annotate missing publication dates from document footers, NORC announcements, or file metadata.
